questions.question: Explain how Distance Vector Routing (DVR) works.
expectedPoints: DVR uses a distance vector to determine the best path for forwarding packets.
Each node maintains a distance vector that contains the shortest distance to each destination node.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the advantages and disadvantages of Link State Routing (LSR) and Path Vector Routing (PVR).
expectedPoints: LSR has fast convergence and no routing loops, but high overhead.
PVR has fast convergence and no routing loops, but high overhead.
LSR is more complex to implement than PVR.
